text: What if I told you a simple sugar can transform into alcohol? This is called alcoholic fermentation, a process used for centuries in brewing and baking. It all starts with yeast. This tiny fungus munches on sugar, producing alcohol and carbon dioxide. But not all yeast acts the same. Different strains yield different flavors and alcohol levels. So, next time you enjoy a drink, remember that tiny organisms worked magic behind the scenes. Curious about more science secrets? Like and follow for more!
